name: Muslim Association of Puget Sound
mission: PNW Seminary provides a structured program for adults to deepen their understanding of Islamic scholarship and leadership.
theoryOfChange: Investing in education (scholarships/seminaries) and social services (clinics/assistance) to produce leaders and a stable community that follows the prophetic example of service.
scoreSummary: Muslim Association of Puget Sound shows strong impact and good alignment, with zakat compliance (minor risk deductions applied).

all_citations.claim: The organization reported total revenue of $7,286,362 and a program expense ratio of 81.7%.
MAPS provides PNW Seminary registrations and keeps families from eviction through its social programs.
The organization mobilizes funding for small businesses and serves a large student population across all programs.
MAPS tracks total enrollments and serves a significant number of Friday congregants.
The charity provides food and gas cards, houses women in permanent shelters, and serves patients at a medical clinic.
The organization offers various donation options including Zakat-eligible giving.
MAPS provides engagement opportunities through volunteer transportation and senior services.

baseline_narrative.amal_score_rationale: The organization earns a solid 70/100 due to its high financial efficiency and direct service impact, though its overall score is moderated by medium data confidence.
areas_for_improvement: Enhance data transparency to improve overall data confidence scores.
Expand reporting on long-term longitudinal outcomes for seminary graduates.

dimension_explanations.alignment: The organization shows high donor alignment for those seeking to support religious and cultural causes, offering Zakat-eligible giving options and robust volunteer opportunities [7][8]. Its long track record of serving thousands of congregants and students establishes it as a cornerstone institution in the region [4][5].
dimension_explanations.impact: MAPS demonstrates high direct impact with a cost per beneficiary of approximately $778.64, providing essential services such as iftar meals, medical clinic access, and eviction prevention [2][6]. The organization maintains a high program expense ratio of 81.7%, reflecting a strong commitment to direct service delivery [1].

baseline_narrative.headline: The Muslim Association of Puget Sound serves as a vital hub for Islamic scholarship, leadership development, and comprehensive community support in the Pacific Northwest.
strengths: High program expense ratio of 81.7%, ensuring the vast majority of funds directly support community services.
Exceptional fundraising efficiency, spending only $0.02 to raise every $1.
Strong liquidity position with 17.3 months of working capital available.
Verified Zakat-eligible status for donors seeking to fulfill religious obligations.
baseline_narrative.summary: The Muslim Association of Puget Sound (MAPS) provides a structured environment for adult Islamic education through the PNW Seminary and maintains active religious services including daily prayers [1][2]. The organization reported a total revenue of $7,286,362, supporting a wide range of social services including food assistance, medical care, and housing support [1][6].

confidence.notes: Strong financial efficiency data but governance documentation is developing.

alignment.explanation: The organization is a <cite id="20">verified Zakat-eligible</cite> entity that serves the <cite id="21">fuqara and masakin</cite> (poor and needy) through its community relief programs.
alignment.improvement: Alignment could be further enhanced by <cite id="22">expanding services to underserved geographies</cite> outside the immediate Puget Sound area.

credibility.explanation: Donors can trust MAPS due to its <cite id="15">100% independent board</cite> and its long-standing <cite id="1">presence since 2006</cite>. The organization maintains <cite id="10">high working capital</cite>, suggesting responsible financial management.
credibility.improvement: To strengthen credibility, the organization should <cite id="16">expand its board of directors</cite> beyond the current four members to ensure broader oversight.

impact.explanation: MAPS delivers high-value direct services with a <cite id="18">cost per beneficiary of $778.64</cite>, which is competitive for the global education and religious sector. They have <cite id="19">mobilized $450,000 for small businesses</cite> and provided critical <cite id="7">eviction prevention for 70 families</cite>.
impact.improvement: Impact measurement would benefit from <cite id="17">publishing a clear reserve-use policy</cite> to explain how their 17 months of capital will be deployed for future growth.

ideal_donor_profile.best_for_summary: Best for donors who want to support a established Islamic institution that balances high-level religious scholarship with direct, local humanitarian relief.
donor_motivations: Want to fulfill zakat through a verified local institution
Value the development of Islamic leadership and scholarship in America
Prefer organizations with extremely low fundraising costs
Interested in supporting comprehensive social services like medical clinics and housing
giving_considerations: Gifts support a large, established institution with significant reserves
Focus is primarily domestic (USA) rather than international relief
Organization is in a growth phase with a 20.8% revenue CAGR
ideal_donor_profile.not_ideal_for: Donors seeking to fund international emergency response or those who prioritize organizations with large, multi-national boards.

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: The organization provides detailed annual reports with specific beneficiary counts and financial breakdowns, though it lacks external academic evaluations.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: MAPS creates impact by providing religious education to build community leadership, which then fuels a social service engine that provides direct aid to the vulnerable.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Verified outcomes ensure that donor funds are translating into tangible results like meals served and families housed.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: With a 20.8% growth rate and expanding seminary programs, MAPS has the capacity to scale its social services further.

strengths.detail: The organization directs <cite id="8">81.7% of its total expenses</cite> toward its core programs, including religious services and community relief.
MAPS operates with a fundraising efficiency of <cite id="9">$0.02 per $1 raised</cite>, indicating a highly effective donor engagement strategy with minimal cost.
The charity maintains <cite id="10">17.3 months of working capital</cite>, providing a significant buffer for long-term operational stability.

strengths_deep_dive: MAPS demonstrates a strong commitment to direct service delivery, particularly in crisis intervention and education. Their social service arm, MAPS-MCRC, has achieved significant scale, <cite id="11">housing women in permanent shelters</cite> and <cite id="12">serving patients at their medical clinic</cite>. Educationally, the PNW Seminary provides a structured pathway for <cite id="4">adult Islamic scholarship</cite>, recently seeing <cite id="13">100 new registrations</cite> and reaching <cite id="14">950 total students</cite> across its various programs. This breadth of service is supported by a <cite id="15">100% independent board</cite>, ensuring high standards of governance and accountability.
rich_narrative.summary: The Muslim Association of Puget Sound (MAPS) serves as a cornerstone of the Islamic community in the Pacific Northwest, operating as a multi-functional hub for religious, educational, and social services. Founded in <cite id="1">2006</cite>, the organization has grown into a significant institution with a <cite id="2">3-year revenue CAGR of 20.8%</cite>, reaching a total revenue of <cite id="3">$7,286,362</cite>. MAPS distinguishes itself through a dual-track model: providing high-level Islamic scholarship through the <cite id="4">PNW Seminary</cite> while simultaneously operating a robust social safety net. Their impact is quantifiable across diverse sectors; in recent years, they have <cite id="5">served 20,500 Iftar meals</cite>, provided <cite id="6">75,000 food and gas cards</cite> to those in need, and successfully <cite id="7">kept 70 families from eviction</cite>. The organization maintains a <cite id="8">program expense ratio of 81.7%</cite>, ensuring that the vast majority of donor contributions are directed toward these community-facing services rather than administrative overhead. Furthermore, MAPS demonstrates exceptional fiscal discipline, spending only <cite id="9">$0.01 to raise every $1</cite> in contributions, a metric that significantly outperforms many peer organizations in the religious and educational sectors.
